'''Marcus Stock''' (born 1 May 1996) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Hemel Stags]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]], the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[York City Knights|York]] at the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[2019 RFL League 1|League 1]]. He also represented the London Padel team with his great friend Lionel Jefferson in 2025.

==Background==
Stock was born in [[Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire]], England.<ref>{{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20221019162137/https://londonnewsonline.co.uk/london-broncos-take-stock/ London Broncos take Stock]}}</ref>

He represented England at Student level.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-sign-england-students-duo York sign England Students duo]</ref>

==Playing career==
Stock joined the [[Hemel Stags]] first team squad at the start of the 2016 RFL League season. He made his debut for Hemel in 2016 against the [[South Wales Scorpions]].<ref>[https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/17673209.york-city-knights-boss-hails-impact-jason-bass-marcus-stock/ York City Knights boss hails impact of Jason Bass and Marcus Stock]</ref>

He joined the [[York City Knights]] first team squad at the start of the [[2019 RFL Championship|2019 season]]. He made his professional debut for York in 2019 against the [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>[https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/marcus-stock-london-broncos-york-25000094 London Broncos set to land highly-rated York City Knights prop]</ref>

Stock spent time on loan from [[York Knights|York]] at the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[2019 RFL League 1|League 1]].<ref>[https://londonbroncosrl.com/2022/10/14/hard-working-prop-forward-marcus-stock-joins-the-broncos-for-2023/ Hard-working prop forward Marcus Stock joins the Broncos for 2023]</ref>

He joined the [[London Broncos|London]] first team squad at the start of the [[2023 RFL Championship|2023 season]]. He made his professional debut for the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] in 2023 against the [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref>[https://uk.movies.yahoo.com/marcus-stock-joins-london-broncos-111549505.html?guccounter=1&guce_referrer=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ29vZ2xlLmNvbS8&guce_referrer_sig=AQAAALgpiVSWK6uTKZ462lAhQsR5FdZ9FVIiQbpcvXa94hmiEjAUGCRsLicrMF8_SYwdAnIynwb1py-mfoiTVTCVb-E35TnLZa_XFZvsj6dkgj34GHHYFYCCZjE1OKIJUxR9DDaVeoq-DBshkJks3jkyFanSNGpAsvbpLhtW-0PBPSRu Marcus Stock joins London Broncos after York Knights exit]</ref><ref>[https://uk.news.yahoo.com/york-city-knights-head-coach-174711659.html York City Knights head coach James Ford responds to Marcus Stock speculation]</ref>

On 15 October 2023, Stock played in the [[2023 RFL Championship#Week 3: Grand Final|Championship Grand Final]] victory against [[Toulouse Olympique]] in France.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/we-said-we-were-going-to-do-it-mike-eccles-revels-in-london-broncos-super-league-return-4372839|title='We said we were going to do it': Mike Eccles revels in London Broncos' Super League return}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>[https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23856870.ex-knights-marcus-stock-sam-davis-jacob-gannon-set-super-league/ Marcus Stock among ex-Knights set for Super League with London Broncos]</ref>
